notes: Use aoa-adr-write when the task is to capture a concrete architectural or workflow decision.
Use aoa-source-of-truth-check when the task is to identify which documents are authoritative and reduce overlap.
aoa-bounded-context-map is about naming contexts and interfaces at the system or domain level.
aoa-core-logic-boundary is about separating reusable rules from glue or orchestration.
aoa-port-adapter-refactor is about isolating a concrete dependency behind a port or adapter.
aoa-change-protocol is the generic bounded-change workflow.
aoa-tdd-slice is specifically test-first feature slicing.
aoa-contract-test is specifically about validating a module/service/workflow boundary.
aoa-property-invariants authors new invariant or property checks.
aoa-invariant-coverage-audit inspects whether current tests already prove the relevant invariants.
aoa-session-donor-harvest is the post-session nucleus: it authors a bounded HARVEST_PACKET and names the next honest family handoff.
aoa-checkpoint-closeout-bridge turns checkpoint-led shortlist hints into one explicit reviewed-closeout chain without letting provisional notes become final authority.

aoa-automation-opportunity-scan classifies whether a recurring reviewed route should stay manual, become a bounded skill, or become a playbook automation seed without granting scheduler or background authority.
aoa-session-route-forks makes several plausible next routes legible when continuation should stay branch-aware instead of hidden in one recommendation.
aoa-session-self-diagnose turns repeated friction, drift, or proof blur from reviewed evidence into a diagnosis packet before mutation.
aoa-session-self-repair turns an existing reviewed diagnosis into the smallest honest repair packet with checkpoint posture and rollback thinking.
aoa-session-progression-lift reflects reviewed mastery movement as a bounded multi-axis delta without pretending to be routing or role authority.
aoa-quest-harvest stays the narrow final promotion verdict once the material is already reduced to one repeated reviewed quest unit.
aoa-approval-gate-check classifies risk and whether execution is allowed at all.
aoa-dry-run-first insists on preview or simulation before a real mutation.
aoa-safe-infra-change handles bounded infra or config changes with rollback thinking.
aoa-local-stack-bringup is the local multi-service stack start path and is explicit-only.
aoa-sanitized-share is about turning raw technical material into a safe public artifact.
Use a project overlay only when the repository actually matches that overlay family and the repo-relative adaptation matters.
Current live overlay families here are the atm10-* and abyss-* repo-local exemplars.
Use the base AoA skill when the workflow does not need a thin project overlay.

cases.prompt: Review the current test suite and tell me whether it actually proves the important invariants for this parser, then name the thinnest gaps.
cases.note: This is an audit of existing coverage, not authoring new properties.

cases.prompt: Write invariant-oriented checks for this parser so we verify idempotency and structural preservation across many generated inputs.
cases.note: This authors new property checks instead of auditing existing coverage.

cases.prompt: The terms account, tenant, workspace, and organization are overloaded across this repo. Map the bounded contexts and the interfaces between them before we change code.
cases.note: This is primarily semantic boundary mapping at the context level.

cases.prompt: The discount rules are mixed into orchestration code and cron wiring. Separate reusable business rules from glue without widening the refactor.
cases.note: This is about rule-vs-glue separation, not system context mapping or a dependency seam.

cases.prompt: Our service layer is tightly coupled to the concrete Stripe client. Introduce a narrow port and adapter so tests stop depending on the live SDK shape.
cases.note: This is dependency isolation behind a port and adapter.

cases.prompt: Add the new password-reset behavior test-first: define the expected flow in tests, implement the smallest passing slice, and keep the refactor bounded.
cases.note: This is specifically a test-first feature slice.

cases.prompt: The webhook producer and consumer disagree on payload shape. Add contract-oriented validation so the boundary is explicit and regressions are obvious.
cases.note: Boundary contract validation is the center of gravity here.

cases.prompt: Make a bounded non-trivial repository change across code and docs, verify it, and report exactly what changed and how you checked it.
cases.note: General bounded change workflow, not specifically test-first or infra-heavy.

cases.prompt: Before touching production DNS records, classify whether this request is safe, approval-gated, or out of bounds.
cases.note: Even though the prompt matches the domain strongly, this skill is explicit-only and should require a manual $skill invocation.

cases.prompt: Find the safest preview path for this delete or restore operation, show what the preview proves, and stop before any real mutation.
cases.note: This skill is explicit-only; implicit routing should not auto-select it.

cases.prompt: Plan and verify a bounded Kubernetes configuration change with rollback thinking and proportional checks.
cases.note: Strong semantic match, but implicit routing must stay off because this skill is explicit-only.

cases.prompt: Show what this docker-compose plus background worker stack would start, run host readiness checks, then launch it through one explicit entrypoint.
cases.note: This is an explicit-only operational skill and should not auto-trigger.

cases.prompt: Turn these raw logs and config snippets into a public-safe incident appendix while keeping the raw material separate.
cases.note: This skill is explicit-only because sanitized sharing is safety-sensitive and should be invoked intentionally.

cases.prompt: Inside this atm10-billing repo, apply the base change protocol but use repo-relative atm10 paths, local commands, and local approval notes.
cases.note: The overlay should win only when the repo is actually an atm10-* repo and the project-specific overlay matters.

cases.prompt: Apply the bounded change workflow in this generic service repo. There is no atm10 overlay or project-specific path layer here.
cases.note: The base skill should win when no ATM10 overlay is needed.

cases.prompt: Inside this atm10-orders repo, map the authoritative docs using repo-relative atm10 canonical-file patterns and local review posture.
cases.note: Overlay-specific source-of-truth mapping inside an ATM10 repo.

cases.prompt: This repo has three conflicting architecture guides and two outdated runbooks. Identify which files are authoritative and shorten the entrypoints into a link-driven map.
cases.note: Document authority mapping for a generic repo, not an overlay-specific ATM10 case, a decision record task, or a post-session quest-promotion triage pass.

cases.prompt: Inside this abyss-stack repo, plan a bounded infra change with repo-relative abyss paths, explicit local authority, and rollback thinking.
cases.note: This overlay still requires an explicit $skill invocation even when the repo-specific match is strong.

cases.prompt: Inside this abyss-stack repo, place a sanitized incident artifact in the repo-relative abyss sharing surface with explicit local thresholds.
cases.note: This overlay still requires an explicit $skill invocation even when the repo-specific match is strong.

cases.prompt: Turn this reviewed session recap into a bounded HARVEST_PACKET, route reusable units to the right owner layer, and name whether the next honest handoff is automation scan, forks, diagnosis, progression, or quest promotion.
cases.note: This is the family nucleus: harvest first, then hand off. Even with a strong semantic match it stays explicit-only.

cases.prompt: The reviewed session is already harvested, but there are three materially different next routes with different risks and owner targets, including staying manual versus lifting one branch into an automation seed. Surface explicit fork cards instead of burying the choice in one recommendation.
cases.note: This is branch analysis after review, not first-pass harvest, automation-readiness classification, diagnosis, or final quest promotion.

cases.prompt: Across the reviewed session notes, the same blockers, proof gaps, hidden inputs, and approval blur keep recurring. Turn that evidence into a diagnosis packet before anyone claims the route is automation-ready or starts patching.
cases.note: Diagnosis is the next honest move here, not donor extraction, automation-readiness classification, repair execution, or progression reflection.

cases.prompt: We already have a reviewed diagnosis packet. Turn it into the smallest repair packet with checkpoint posture, rollback marker, health check, and owner target so later automation claims rest on a safer boundary.
cases.note: This is repair planning after diagnosis, not diagnosis itself, broader donor harvest, branch analysis, or automation-readiness classification.

cases.prompt: The reviewed session clearly moved several mastery axes in different directions. Emit a bounded progression delta with honest advance, hold, or reanchor signals and small unlock hints.
cases.note: This is progression reflection from reviewed evidence, not donor harvest, diagnosis, or quest promotion.

cases.prompt: The material is already reduced to one repeated reviewed quest unit and the only real question is whether it should stay a quest or be promoted into the next owner surface.
cases.note: This is the narrow final promotion verdict, not wider session-family harvest, fork routing, diagnosis, or progression reflection.

cases.prompt: This reviewed workflow keeps recurring across sessions with the same inputs, the same approval seam, and the same expected output. Classify whether it should stay manual, become a bounded skill, or become a playbook automation seed with checkpoint requirements called out explicitly.
cases.note: This is automation-readiness classification for a recurring route, not donor extraction, branch comparison, diagnosis-first analysis, or repair planning.

cases.prompt: Inside this abyss-stack repo, gather repo-relative runtime evidence around one concrete target path, normalize it into a bounded diagnostic session artifact, and name the honest exit class before any repair claim is made.
cases.note: The abyss overlay should win when the base diagnosis workflow needs one runtime-owned repo-relative artifact instead of a generic session packet.

cases.prompt: This abyss repo now has a diagnostic spine note, runbook fragments, and overlay guidance that disagree about which file is authoritative. Map the canonical docs and shorten the entrypoints instead of producing a runtime artifact.
cases.note: When the real problem is document authority around the diagnostic spine, source-of-truth mapping should win and the overlay should defer.

cases.prompt: A reviewed session artifact, a checkpoint note, and a reviewed closeout handoff already exist. Build one explicit closeout context, reread the full reviewed artifact, and drive donor harvest, progression lift, and quest harvest in that order.
cases.note: This is the explicit reviewed-closeout bridge: checkpoint hints stay provisional, but the bridge itself should win when the task is to coordinate the full donor -> progression -> quest chain.

cases.prompt: Implement a bounded code-and-docs patch across aoa-sdk and aoa-skills so the checkpoint closeout bridge becomes real, then verify the repo batteries and report what changed.
cases.note: When the task is to implement bridge support rather than execute a reviewed closeout chain, the bounded change workflow should win and the bridge skill should defer.
